dimensions.gap: Common middleware discovery, invalid request smoke coverage, and request binding examples are less immediately visible than mature HTTP-first frameworks.

dimensions.gap: Generated upgrade trust is now documented and gated, but adopter-facing scaffold decisions still need one consolidated comparison and migration path.

dimensions.gap: RPC, gateway, and app remain Tier 2 until unary/stream benchmarks, resolver/balancer smoke, and Kitex rollback evidence are machine-indexed.
dimensions.todo: Create a Tier 1 promotion evidence manifest and gate for rpc/gateway/app.

dimensions.gap: Production assets exist, but the reference app topology is not yet indexed as one full REST/RPC/MQ/outbox/cache/discovery/observability/K8s proof.

dimensions.gap: Release evidence is indexed and gated; the next gap is publishing it as a concise adopter-facing release readiness score.

dimensions.gap: Plugin protocol has conformance coverage, but third-party template publishing and permission-review UX remain early compared with mature extension ecosystems.

dimensions.gap: Bench governance exists and selected OpenAPI latency rows are now blocking; the remaining latency-critical HTTP and RPC rows still need more trend confidence before promotion.
dimensions.todo: Continue promoting hot-path latency budgets from report-only to blocking as trend confidence grows.

dimensions.gap: Adopter guidance exists, but top-level framework comparison needs one machine-validated TODO matrix to steer future work.
